paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Jehovah spake unto Moses, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Send thou men, that they may spy out the land of Canaan, which I give unto the children of Israel: of every tribe of their fathers shall ye send a man, every one a prince among them.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses sent them from the wilderness of Paran according to the commandment of Jehovah: all of them men who were heads of the children of Israel.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And these were their names: Of the tribe of Reuben, Shammua the son of Zaccur.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat the son of Hori.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb the son of Jephunneh.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal the son of Joseph.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Hoshea the son of Nun.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Palti the son of Raphu.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel the son of Sodi.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Joseph, [namely], of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi the son of Susi.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel the son of Gemalli.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Asher, Sethur the son of Michael.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi the son of Vophsi.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Of the tribe of Gad, Geuel the son of Machi.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: These are the names of the men that Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses called Hoshea the son of Nun Joshua.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, and said unto them, Get you up this way by the South, and go up into the hill-country:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and see the land, what it is; and the people that dwell therein, whether they are strong or weak, whether they are few or many;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and what the land is that they dwell in, whether it is good or bad; and what cities they are that they dwell in, whether in camps, or in strongholds;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: and what the land is, whether it is fat or lean, whether there is wood therein, or not. And be ye of good courage, and bring of the fruit of the land. Now the time was the time of the first-ripe grapes.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: So they went up, and spied out the land from the wilderness of Zin unto Rehob, to the entrance of Hamath.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they went up by the South, and came unto Hebron; and Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the children of Anak, were there. (Now Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they came unto the valley of Eshcol, and cut down from thence a branch with one cluster of grapes, and they bare it upon a staff between two; [they brought] also of the pomegranates, and of the figs.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: That place was called the valley of Eshcol, because of the cluster which the children of Israel cut down from thence.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they returned from spying out the land at the end of forty days.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they went and came to Moses, and to Aaron, and to all the congregation of the children of Israel, unto the wilderness of Paran, to Kadesh; and brought back word unto them, and unto all the congregation, and showed them the fruit of the land.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they told him, and said, We came unto the land whither thou sentest us; and surely it floweth with milk and honey; and this is the fruit of it.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Howbeit the people that dwell in the land are strong, and the cities are fortified, [and] very great: and moreover we saw the children of Anak there.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Amalek dwelleth in the land of the South: and the Hittite, and the Jebusite, and the Amorite, dwell in the hill-country; and the Canaanite dwelleth by the sea, and along by the side of the Jordan.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Caleb stilled the people before Moses, and said, Let us go up at once, and possess it; for we are well able to overcome it.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But the men that went up with him said, We are not able to go up against the people; for they are stronger than we.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And they brought up an evil report of the land which they had spied out unto the children of Israel, saying, The land, through which we have gone to spy it out, is a land that eateth up the inhabitants thereof; and all the people that we saw in it are men of great stature.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And there we saw the Nephilim, the sons of Anak, who come of the Nephilim: and we were in our own sight as grasshoppers, and so we were in their sight.
